mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in retail sales signals resilient nominal household demand and stronger expected corporate revenues, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot was 724131.0 as of two months before 2025-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month RSAFS trends do not produce higher average 20-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends over a 3-year walk-forward window, or if Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate crosses above 4.0% and continues rising over 3 months, tighter discount-rate conditions reduce equity valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS crosses above 4.0% and 3-month change in FEDFUNDS is positive
evidence_refs: fact:latest FEDFUNDS snapshot was 4.33 as of one month before 2025-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered observations produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of walk-forward cases, or if returns are not lower than unconditional returns at p < 0.10.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in unemployment signals deteriorating labor income and recession risk, lowering expected earnings and producing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises more than 0.3 percentage points over 3 months
evidence_refs: fact:latest UNRATE snapshot was 4.2 as of one month before 2025-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered unemployment increases are followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of cases, or if the triggered mean return is not below the unconditional mean.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating CPI while the federal funds rate is above 4.0% signals persistent inflation pressure without policy relief, increasing real-income and discount-rate headwinds and producing negative 20-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL 3-month change accelerates versus its prior 3-month change while FEDFUNDS is above 4.0%
evidence_refs: fact:latest CPIAUCSL snapshot was 320.580 and FEDFUNDS was 4.33
failure_condition: Reject if the joint CPI acceleration and high FEDFUNDS condition does not have lower 20-day returns than CPI acceleration alone, or if interaction-term Rank IC is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A positive 3-month trend in industrial production signals improving real output and operating leverage, raising expected earnings and producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
evidence_refs: fact:latest INDPRO snapshot was 103.8799 as of two months before 2025-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if positive 3-month INDPRO trends fail to predict higher 60-day S&P 500 returns than negative trends in at least 55% of walk-forward windows.

mechanism_narrative: A drop in housing starts below their 12-month average with negative 6-month momentum signals weakening rate-sensitive demand and future construction activity, producing negative 60-day S&P 500 returns.
candidate_trigger: HOUST crosses below its 12-month average and 6-month change in HOUST is negative
evidence_refs: fact:latest HOUST snapshot was 1361.0 as of two months before 2025-06-15
failure_condition: Reject if triggered HOUST weakness is followed by positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 60% of observations, or if the downside hit rate is not above baseline.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll growth with a stable unemployment rate signals a soft-landing labor market in which income supports demand without recession stress, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month average change is positive and UNRATE 3-month change is less than or equal to 0.1 percentage points
evidence_refs: fact:latest PAYEMS snapshot was 159561 and UNRATE was 4.2
failure_condition: Reject if the joint payroll-growth and stable-unemployment condition does not outperform payroll growth alone over 20 trading days, or if its walk-forward mean excess return is nonpositive.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth combined with decelerating CPI signals improving real consumption and reduced inflation risk, causing a short-horizon relief bid in equities and positive 5-day S&P 500 forward returns.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS 3-month change is positive and CPIAUCSL 3-month change is lower than its prior 3-month change
evidence_refs: fact:latest RSAFS snapshot was 724131.0 and CPIAUCSL was 320.580
failure_condition: Reject if the joint retail-sales growth and CPI deceleration condition fails to produce positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in at least 55% of triggered observations.
